paper.title: Gaia DR2 in 6D: Searching for the fastest stars in the Galaxy

extraction.summary: Extracted 125 high-velocity star candidates (Pub>0.5) from Gaia DR2 6D search by Marchetti et al. (2019). 20 candidates with Pub>0.8 (7 Galactic HRS, 13 extragalactic), 105 with 0.5<Pub<=0.8. No HVS candidates found.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 catalog: 7,183,262 stars with 6D phase-space (positions, parallaxes, proper motions, radial velocities)

source_refs.context: Gaia DR2: 1.3B with astrometry, 7.2M with radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Two-tier distance inference: low-f (f<=0.1) simple inversion d=1/varpi with Gaussian MC; high-f (f>0.1 or negative varpi) Bayesian with exponentially decreasing prior (L=2600 pc) using emcee MCMC with 32 walkers, 100 steps
inputs: Exponentially decreasing prior P(d) ~ d^2 exp(-d/L)

source_refs.context: Low-f: simple inversion. High-f: full Bayesian with exponentially decreasing prior
Posterior P(d|varpi) with prior ~ d^2 exp(-d/L), L=2600 pc

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 radial velocities from RVS, assumed Gaussian with sigma_vrad, uncorrelated with astrometry

source_refs.context: RVS radial velocities: ~few hundred m/s bright end, few km/s faint end

method_chain.summary: Galactocentric total velocity v_GC from MC samples, corrected for solar motion (d_sun=8.2 kpc, v_LSR=238 km/s, U,V,W=[14.0,12.24,7.25] km/s)

source_refs.context: v_GC from MC sampling, solar/LSR corrections applied

method_chain.summary: Gala MilkyWayPotential: Hernquist bulge+nuclus, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, NFW halo (Bovy 2015 parameters)

source_refs.context: Four-component Galactic potential for escape velocity

method_chain.summary: Quality cuts: astrometric_gof_al<3, astrometric_excess_noise_sig<=2, -0.23<=mean_varpi_factor_al<=0.32, visibility_periods_used>8, rv_nb_transits>5, sigma_vGC/vGC<0.3

source_refs.context: Quality cuts on Gaia DR2 columns

method_chain.summary: Unbound probability Pub = fraction of MC realizations with v_GC > v_esc(r). High-velocity defined as Pub>0.5 (125 stars after quality cuts).

source_refs.context: Pub = fraction of MC where v_GC > escape speed
125 clean high-velocity stars with Pub>0.5

method_chain.summary: Backward orbit integration up to 5 Gyr with Gala MilkyWayPotential, 1000 MC samples per star, tracking disk crossings and r_min
outputs: r_min, v_ej, eccentricity, Z_max, energy distribution

source_refs.context: Orbit integration back in time, 1000 MC samples
Disk crossing tracking, r_min defined at z_GC=0

method_chain.summary: Milky Way origin probability PMW = fraction of MC with r_min<25 kpc. PMW>0.5 = Galactic (HRS candidates), PMW<0.5 = extragalactic. HVS test: r_min-sigma<1 kpc (none found).

source_refs.context: PMW = fraction with r_min<25 kpc. PMW<0.5 = extragalactic
HVS: r_min-sigma<1 kpc, none found

inclusion_assessment.summary: Gaia DR2 5932173855446728064: Pub=1.0, PMW=1.00, classified as Galactic HRS candidate in Marchetti et al. (2019). v_GC=747^+2_-3 km/s.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes Pub>0.8 using MC sampling of Gaia DR2 astrometry with MilkyWayPotential escape velocity.
